ops.title: AGENTS.md guide content ships via cc-guides embedded fragments (auto-release on push to main) + cc-skills guides/ fan-out — the old repo-bootstrap _partials stamp/sync is dead
ops.body:
As of 2026-07-09 the stamped-AGENTS.md mechanism is the `cc-guides` Go binary (github.com/yasyf/cc-guides, sibling checkout), NOT the repo-bootstrap `_partials/*.md` + stamp/sync/drift Python scripts (those were mid-deletion, uncommitted, in cc-skills' tree during the v3 cutover — never resurrect them).

- Canonical fragment content lives in TWO byte-identical copies: `cc-guides/fragments/md/*.md` (go:embed'd into the binary — this is what actually renders) and `cc-skills/guides/md/*.md` (the "content home"). Edit BOTH; `cmp` to verify.
- Consumers render `AGENTS.src.md` → `AGENTS.md` via `{{> name}}` directives; commands are `cc-guides render` / `check` (no `sync` subcommand). One banner stamp at the top of the file, no per-fragment envelopes.
- Propagation: cc-guides releases automatically on EVERY push to main (release-go.yml auto-tags `v0.1.<run_number>` — no manual tag). Deliberately no freshness self-check in the release: a fragment edit would deadlock its own release. Then a push to cc-skills `guides/**`/`fleet.json` fires `guides-fan-out.yml`, dispatching `cc-guides-render` to every repo in `fleet.json` (21 repos incl. cc-context and cc-guides itself); each installs the LATEST release and commits a re-render. Weekly cron (`17 9 * * 1`) is the belt. Order matters: land the cc-guides release BEFORE pushing the cc-skills content commit.
- Local brew `cc-guides` goes stale immediately after a release — never hand-run `cc-guides render` in a consumer to "help"; the fleet loop with the fresh binary does it, and a stale local render regresses content.
- 2026-07-09: ccx fragment updated this way (grep `-i/-w/--scope` + guard-pack rewrite-first wording) — cc-guides commit 413e8c24, cc-skills commit 618e8220. [[ccx-grep-engine-guard-rewrites]] [[cc-skills-plugin-source]] [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]]
- jj trap hit during this: in a shared working copy based one commit behind main, `jj commit <fileset>` carves the commit onto the STALE base; fix with `jj rebase -r <change> -d main`, then address the change by CHANGE id (the commit id goes obsolete after rebase) for `jj bookmark set`.
